title: Digital State: Sarawak adopts AI to address citizen needs
summary: Sarawak is moving from digitalisation into early AI adoption across public services, with Dayang as a citizen-facing AI assistant and SAIC coordinating policy and adoption. The article frames AI as a way to improve services, productivity, inclusion, and data-driven governance while noting readiness gaps.
why_it_matters: This is the strongest baseline context item for the brief: it explains the institutions, policy frame, and adoption barriers behind Sarawak's AI push.
caveat: Use as background analysis; verify article date/access before quoting.

title: Sarawak establishing Quantum AI research, economics collaboration with Cambridge University, UK
summary: Sarawak is positioning AI as a strategic capability through research collaboration, sovereign infrastructure, and applied AI projects across government-linked institutions. The report cites Sarawak Cloud, a sovereign AI platform, AI Grid work with SAINS, and sector applications in biodiversity, forestry, agriculture, energy, and finance.
why_it_matters: This moves Sarawak AI from generic adoption talk into a concrete institutional roadmap: research partnerships plus local AI infrastructure plus applied projects.
caveat: Official-speech context; follow-up sources should check implementation progress.

title: AI to transform Sarawak's economy, services, workforce productivity
summary: Professor Patrick Then of the Sarawak AI Centre frames AI as a productivity multiplier for public services, business operations, and administrative workflows. The article uses DeepSAR and SKAS eligibility/form-filling as examples while flagging digital divide and implementation-readiness concerns.
why_it_matters: This provides the public-facing adoption story: AI as service acceleration and workforce capacity expansion, not just infrastructure strategy.
caveat: Extractor summary was truncated; revisit source before direct quotation.

title: SDEC News listing shows AI, deep-tech, IoT and startup pipeline items
summary: SDEC's news listing includes recurring digital-economy programme announcements, including a TFI demo day with AI, cybersecurity and IoT teams, startup showcases, and digital entrepreneurship programmes. This makes SDEC a core recurring source for the watchlist.
why_it_matters: A recurring official source is needed for a sustainable brief; SDEC appears to be one of the highest-yield institutional feeds.
caveat: Listing page only; individual articles need extraction before inclusion in a published brief.
